steps.m: Special letters that begin this chapter (their full meaning is known only to Allah)
steps.n: This is a special combination of letters that appears at the start of this chapter. While we don't know their exact meaning, they remind us that the Quran is a divine miracle.
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'that which is recited'. The ء helps pronounce the long vowel sound.
Adding 'al' makes it refer specifically to the Holy Quran
Adding 'wa' connects it to what came before with 'and'
The kasra at the end shows this is being sworn by, as it follows an implied oath phrase 'I swear by...'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who has this quality strongly
Added ال with hamzat wasl (ٱ) to make it definite. This word describes the Quran in this ayah.

steps.m: The All-Wise (describing القرءان, connected to وَٱلْقُرْءَانِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to وَٱلْقُرْءَانِ (and by the Quran) in the ayah
This is a common particle used at the start of sentences to add emphasis to what comes after it
Added كَ at the end which means 'you' when speaking to one male. In this ayah, it's addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, as the next words لَمِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ confirm he is among the messengers.

steps.m: from/among (shows where something starts or what group it belongs to)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or what group something belongs to

steps.m: surely from/among (emphasizing that something definitely belongs to a group)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to show emphasis. This is connected to إِنَّكَ (indeed you) before it, making the whole phrase mean 'indeed you are surely among...'

steps.m: surely from/among (emphasizing that something definitely belongs to a group, connecting to الْمُرْسَلِينَ)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect this word to الْمُرْسَلِينَ (the messengers) that comes after it, showing you are among that group

steps.m: to send or deliver a message

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he sent' in the past
Added مُ at the beginning to show someone receiving the action, like changing 'send' to 'sent one' or 'messenger'
Added ين to make it plural and show it comes after the word مِنَ (from/among)
Added ال to specify these particular messengers
This is the basic form meaning something high or elevated

steps.m: upon, on top of (pointing to صِرٰطٍ - path)
steps.n: Adding ى changes this from a concept of height into a preposition showing something is on or above something else. Here it shows the subject is upon/on a path (صِرٰطٍ).

steps.m: upon, on top of (pointing to صِرٰطٍ - path)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound, not as a 'y' sound
The pattern فِعَال is used for nouns describing paths and ways
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show it's indefinite and follows عَلَىٰ. Also changed the alif (ا) to a long alif (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation.
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُسْتَ at the start to show someone who actively tries to be straight/upright
Added ي in middle to show this is a lasting, strong quality of being straight/upright
Added shadda on م and genitive case ending because it describes صِرٰطٍ (path)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The form تفعيل is used to show something happening repeatedly or gradually

steps.m: the sending down (possessed by العَزِيزِ الرَّحِيمِ - the Mighty, the Merciful)
steps.n: The fatḥa at the end shows this word is possessed by the words that follow it: العَزِيزِ الرَّحِيمِ (the Mighty, the Merciful)
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to show this is a permanent quality

steps.m: The Mighty (as a name of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite and shows this is one of Allah's names
The kasra ending shows this word is connected to الرَّحِيمِ (The Most Merciful) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
This pattern shows someone who consistently shows this quality
Adding ال makes it specific and in this divine context elevates the meaning to 'Most'
The kasra shows this word is describing العزيز (The Mighty) which comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the verb cause someone to be warned. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
Changed from 'he warned' to 'you warn' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: so that you warn (قَوْمًا - a people)
steps.n: Adds purpose/reason meaning ('in order to')
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning a group of people who stand together (like a nation or tribe)

steps.m: a people (object of لِتُنذِرَ - for you to warn)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being warned. This connects to the verb لِتُنذِرَ (for you to warn) where 'people' are receiving the warning.
This is a particle used for negation or as a relative pronoun meaning 'what'
Added emphasis through shadda on م and lengthening the alif. This emphasizes the connection between what was warned to their forefathers and what is being warned now.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at the start to make someone do the warning (like 'made someone warn' instead of just 'warned')
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: to nurture/care for (as a father does)

steps.n: Added fatha to make it a noun meaning father
Changed to plural form by adding long alif after first letter and اء at end
The word 'fathers' gets a waw at the end because it's connected to a pronoun
Added pronoun هُمْ to show possession (their)
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' (referring to the people whose forefathers were not warned)
Added damma and sukun to show proper pronunciation
Added فَ (so/then) to connect this with the previous statement about their forefathers not being warned

steps.m: to ignore or be unaware of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from describing an action to describing someone who does that action
Makes the word refer to multiple people (3 or more men) who are unaware

steps.m: those who are unaware/ignoring (plural, in subject position)
steps.n: Adds a fatha mark to show this word is in the subject position in the sentence
Basic form of the emphasis particle قَدْ which shows certainty or completion
Added لَ at the beginning to make the emphasis even stronger. This word emphasizes the verb حَقَّ (became true) that comes after it.
Adding the fatha vowel mark shows this is a past tense verb
The root حَقق became حَقَّ by combining two ق letters into one with a shadda, showing something became definitely true in the past. It tells us what happened to قَوْلُ (the word/saying).
Changed from action to noun form. This word is what became true/established (حَقَّ) in this ayah.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action (being established/true)
Basic preposition showing something being on top of, over, or against something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is a spelling variation showing the long 'aa' sound at the end

steps.m: upon/over (referring to أَكْثَرِهِمْ - most of them)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'most of them' (أَكْثَرِهِمْ)
Changed root 'to be abundant' into comparative form 'more/most' and added kasra to connect with هِمْ (their)
Adds 3rd person masculine plural possessive suffix
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they' and sukun (ْ) shows where to stop
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (they do not believe).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it opposite. In this ayah, it negates يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) to mean 'they do not believe'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Form IV pattern changes the meaning to actively choosing to believe, especially in a religious context
Changed to present tense with يُ prefix and internal vowel changes. The hamza changes form due to pronunciation rules.
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
This word makes what comes after it more certain - like adding an exclamation mark in English!
Adding ا at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This connects to the next word جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed) where we are doing the action.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb. This word follows إِنَّا (indeed we) in the ayah.
Adding نا changes the subject to 'we', matching with إِنَّا (indeed we) at the start of the ayah
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that the chains/shackles are physically in/around their necks.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.
Adding damma vowels turns the root into a common noun meaning 'neck'
Changes the word to show many necks using a special plural pattern
Adding genitive case marker
Adds هِمْ at the end to show these necks belong to them
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show basic past tense meaning
Changed from a verb (to chain) into a plural noun meaning multiple chains - like turning 'chain' into 'chains'
The word changed to show chains/shackles as the object being used (like saying 'with chains' instead of just 'chains')
Starting particle that shows this is a result of the previous statement about chains on their necks

steps.m: and so it [the chain] (connects to مُّقْمَحُونَ)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun 'it' referring back to the chains (أَغْلٰلًا) mentioned before. The word مُّقْمَحُونَ (with heads raised) comes after to complete the meaning.
Basic particle showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows the direction 'to the chins'
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Changed to broken plural form by adding أ at the start and ا after second letter. This is a common pattern for making plurals in Arabic.
Added ال to make it definite ('the') and ِ at the end because it comes after the word إِلَى (towards/to) in the ayah
Connecting word showing result or sequence
Added pronoun 'they' which is the subject of the next word مُّقْمَحُونَ (raised up/lifted)

steps.m: to lift up one's head and look upward

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

steps.m: one whose head is lifted up (against their will)
steps.n: Changed to passive participle form to show someone being affected by the action

steps.m: one who is forced to lift their head up
steps.n: The doubled م shows the action is done to someone against their will

steps.m: those who are forcibly made to lift their heads (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in أَعْنٰقِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show this applies to multiple people. This refers back to the disbelievers whose necks were mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. The ْ (sukun) is added on ل to help connect with نَا
Added وَ at start to connect this action with what came before it in the verse
Basic preposition showing the starting point of something. In this ayah, it works with بَيْنِ to mean 'from between'
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end because it comes before ب in بَيْنِ. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Basic concept of separation or distinction between things
Changed from root meaning 'separate' to preposition meaning 'between' by adding vowel marks
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the word after it (أَيْدِيهِمْ) meaning 'their hands'
Changed from root meaning 'to extend' into the word for 'hand' - the body part we use to reach for things
Changed to plural form showing multiple hands or powers
Makes it a complete plural noun meaning multiple hands or powers
Added هِمْ to show possession - 'their'

steps.m: to block or create a barrier

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter to show strength and turned it into a noun

steps.m: a strong barrier (object of جَعَلْنَا - we made/placed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence - they placed a barrier
Basic preposition that connects to the word خَلْفِهِمْ (behind them) to show location
Added وَ to connect this phrase to the previous statement about putting a barrier in front of them. Now it shows they are surrounded by barriers both in front and behind.

steps.m: to be behind or follow after something

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a simple word meaning 'behind' or 'after'

steps.m: behind them (after سَدًّا/barrier in this ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ with kasra (ِ) before it to show possession. This word comes after سَدًّا (barrier) in the ayah, showing there was a barrier behind them.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show a strong, firm barrier
Added tanween alif to show this is the thing being placed/made by جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed)
Changed root letters into past tense verb 'he covered' by adding the vowel marks
Added أ at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُمْ to show who was affected by the action
Added فَ to show this action happened as a result of what came before
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of men or mixed group. It's the subject of the verb 'they do not see' in this ayah.
The فَ shows this happens as a result of what came before - because Allah put barriers in front and behind them and covered them, they cannot see.

This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُبْصِرُونَ (they see) negative, changing its meaning to 'they do not see'. The fatha (َ) is added to show it's being used as a complete word rather than just letters.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show this is happening now. The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) are part of the present tense pattern.

steps.m: they see (subject of لَا - 'they do not see')
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people (they) are doing the action. This word comes after لَا which negates it, making the meaning 'they do not see'

steps.m: to be the same or equal

steps.n: The root letters were connected and given vowel marks to make a word meaning 'equal'. The last letter ي becomes ا at the end of words like this.
Added a long alif and hamza at the end to turn it from an adjective into a noun meaning 'something equal'
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is indefinite and in the subject position

steps.m: and it is equal (subject of عَلَيْهِمْ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'. This word is connected to عَلَيْهِمْ (upon them) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing something being above or on top of something else
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group of people). This word connects to ءَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ (did you warn them) in the ayah.

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the warning more direct and emphatic
Added تَ at the end and sukun on ر to show 'you' did the action
Added هُمْ to show who received the warning
Added ءَ at beginning to make it a question
This is a question particle that means 'or' when connecting two options in a question. Here it connects to the previous question 'Have you warned them?' to ask 'or have you not warned them?'
This is the base form of the negative particle
Adding م with sukun (ْ) makes this particle specifically negate past actions. In this ayah, it negates تُنذِرْ (warn), making it mean 'did not warn'.

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he warned' to 'you warn' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra (i sound)
Changed to jussive mood because it comes after لَمْ in the ayah
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being warned
This is a basic word that means 'not'. It makes the word after it negative, like adding 'not' before a word in English. Here it makes 'they believe' into 'they do not believe'.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding the long alif changes the meaning from just feeling safe to actively believing in something
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Adding shadda makes the word stronger, changing it from 'if' to 'indeed'
Adding ما changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This word restricts the meaning of the verb تُنذِرُ (you warn), showing that the warning is limited to certain people.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The damma at the end shows this is a normal statement (not a command or wish)
This is a basic word used to refer to people in questions or statements. It's like saying 'who' or 'whoever' in English.
Added kasra at the end because this word connects to the verb after it (اتَّبَعَ meaning 'follows'). It's like saying 'whoever follows...'

steps.m: to follow closely or pursue something

steps.n: Added the basic past tense pattern by connecting the letters with fatha marks
Added ت with shadda and helper alif to show the action affects the doer
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: remembrance/mention (the act or result of remembering/mentioning)
steps.n: Changed vowels to turn the verb into a noun that means either the act itself or what results from it
Added ال to make the noun definite, like changing 'remembrance' to 'the remembrance'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being followed in the phrase 'followed the remembrance'
Added vowels to show past tense feeling. This word shows who feared the Most Merciful (الرَّحْمٰنَ) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about following the reminder
Creates an intensive form showing complete and perfect mercy
Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb خَشِىَ (feared) in the ayah

steps.m: The Most Merciful (being feared in خَشِىَ ٱلرَّحْمٰنَ)
steps.n: Adds ال to make it definite, specifically referring to Allah. The ل gets a shadda (ّ) because it comes after the connecting hamza (ٱ)
Changes from an action to a noun - from 'being hidden' to 'the hidden/unseen'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific unseen realm rather than any unseen thing
Added بِ to show how someone relates to the unseen realm. The ال changes to ٱل when connected to بِ.

steps.m: in/with the unseen realm (object of خَشِىَ - fears)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this word is connected to the verb خَشِىَ (fears) through the word بِ
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show doing something a lot or with emphasis
Adding هُ at the end shows who received the good news (him)

steps.m: then he gave him good news
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happened after something else
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes the verb 'he forgave' into the noun 'forgiveness' by adding ma- at start and -ah at end

steps.m: with forgiveness (connected to فَبَشِّرْهُ - then give glad tidings)
steps.n: The بِ shows this is connected to the verb فَبَشِّرْهُ (then give glad tidings), explaining what to give glad tidings about

steps.m: to give a reward or payment for work

steps.n: Changed the verb (giving reward) into a noun (the reward itself)
Added tanween (the ٍ mark) to show this is any reward, not a specific one
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this reward with the forgiveness (مَغْفِرَةٍ) mentioned before it
Added vowels and ي to show this describes a strong quality. This word describes the reward (أَجْرٍ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite and matches the grammatical case of أَجْرٍ (reward) that it describes
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger to show extra emphasis.

steps.m: indeed we (subject of نُحْىِ - we give life)
steps.n: The suffix ا changes this from just 'indeed' to 'indeed we'. This word connects to the verb نُحْىِ (we give life) that comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: we (subject of نُحْىِ - we give life)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'we'. In this ayah, it emphasizes that it is specifically 'we' (Allah) who gives life to the dead, as shown by the verb نُحْىِ (we give life) that follows it.
Connected the root letters together. The meaning shifts from 'being alive' to 'making something alive' because this word will be used as a causative verb.
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: we give life (verb in نَحْنُ نُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ - we give life to the dead)
steps.n: The double ي at the end changes to ى with kasra (ِ) due to being a weak letter at the end of the word. Added sukun (ْ) on ح for proper pronunciation. This verb is part of the phrase 'we give life to the dead'.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for death
Added ى at the end to make it refer to multiple dead things or people
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the dead ones' instead of just 'dead ones'
وَ means 'and', connecting this to the previous statement
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it present tense - sukun on first letter, dammas on others
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: and we write/record (what they sent forth)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with what came before in the verse

steps.m: what (that which they have sent ahead)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that refers to the deeds that people have sent ahead (done in their lives). It connects to قَدَّمُوا (they sent ahead) which comes right after it in the ayah.
These are the basic letters that give us the core meaning of moving or going forward
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb about one person
Doubling the middle letter makes the action more intense and shows someone is causing the action to happen
Adding وا at the end makes it about a group of people instead of just one person
وَ means 'and'
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'trace' by adding the vowel marks a-a
Changed to plural form by adding alif at start and after second root letter
Added و (and) at beginning and changed آ to ءَا with long alif

steps.m: and their traces/footprints (object of وَنَكْتُبُ - and We write)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show possession (their). This word is what is being written (object) in the phrase 'and We write'
Added damma and shadda to create the word meaning 'every/all'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb أَحْصَيْنٰهُ (we have recorded it)
Added وَ (and) to connect this word to what came before it in the verse
Basic noun pattern forming the word for 'thing'. This is one of the most common words in Arabic.
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word is owned by كُلَّ (every/all) that comes before it. Also changed ي to ى which is just a spelling variation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning to show completeness of the action
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding suffixes
Added نا to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we have enumerated it (refers to كُلَّ شَىْءٍ - everything)
steps.n: Added هُ (meaning 'it') to show we counted everything mentioned
This is a preposition showing that something is contained or recorded in something else. In this ayah, it shows that things are recorded in a clear record.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to lead or be in front

steps.n: Changed the أ to إِ and added ا after the first letter to show who does the action. This pattern is used for people who do something as their main role.

steps.m: a clear leader (in فِى a clear record)
steps.n: Added the mark ٍ (called kasratin) to show this word is connected to في (in/within) - like saying 'in a leader'
Connected the root letters together
Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show this describes something that makes things clear
Added shadda on م to make the meaning stronger
Added tanwin to show this describes إِمَامٍ (record) in this ayah

steps.m: to strike or give an example

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adding sukun at the end completes the command form

steps.m: and strike!/give! (an example) (connected to لَهُم - to them)
steps.n: Added 'and' at beginning and changed hamza to wasl form since it's in middle of sentence. This command connects to 'to them' (لَهُم) in the next word.
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'

steps.m: for them/to them (object of وَٱضْرِبْ - and strike)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this is happening to 'them'. This word connects to وَٱضْرِبْ (and strike), showing who should receive the example.
Adding fathas creates a basic noun meaning 'example'
Added emphasis to show this is a clear or striking example

steps.m: a clear example (object of وَٱضْرِبْ - strike/coin)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb وَٱضْرِبْ (strike/coin)
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added alif to make it mean 'one who does the action'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple companions. This word is connected to 'the town' (الْقَرْيَةِ) in this ayah, showing whose companions they were.
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'settlement' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to make it a specific place of settlement
Added ال to specify this particular village
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word 'example' (مَّثَلًا) and shows what the example is about

steps.m: when (introducing the story of أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces a past event. Here it begins the story about the people of the town (أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ) that is being given as an example.
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks

steps.m: came to her (referring to القرية/the town)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to القرية (the town) mentioned earlier in the verse. This shows who was approached.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and made middle letter have sukun to show someone/something receiving the action

steps.m: the messengers (who came to أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ)

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.m: being lost/going astray (as a state or condition)
steps.n: The alif makes this into an abstract noun showing the state of being lost

steps.m: with what/that which (referring to how غَفَرَ - He forgave)

steps.m: and what (that عَمِلَتْهُ their hands made)

steps.m: she made/produced it (referring to what أَيْدِيهِمْ/their hands created)

steps.m: is it true that...? (questioning what follows)

steps.m: so will they not? (expecting them to يَشْكُرُونَ - be grateful)

steps.m: they show gratitude (referring to those who benefit from Allah's provisions mentioned in the previous ayah)

steps.m: to be free from faults/to glorify

steps.n: Added ٱلّ (al with shadda) at the beginning to make it specific and connect it to what was mentioned before (سُبْحٰنَ). The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun meaning one pair

steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of creation mentioned in خَلَقَ (He created)
Added damma and shadda to make the basic word meaning 'all'
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon by the verb خَلَقَ (created)
Added ها to refer back to الْأَزْوٰجَ (the pairs/types) that Allah created

steps.m: to grow or sprout (like a plant)

steps.m: and from/among (connects to تُنۢبِتُ - it grows/produces)

steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase. This word connects to what the earth grows in this ayah.

Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced (la)
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'

Added ون to show this is about a group of people doing the action

steps.m: to show, reveal, or make clear

steps.n: The root letters combine with a long ā sound and end with ة to make a noun meaning 'sign'. Like changing 'show' into 'a showing/sign'.

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form

steps.n: Changed to present tense 'we' form. In this ayah, Allah is describing how He strips away the daylight from the night.

Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)

The و connects this condition with previous statements, showing this is part of a larger sequence of events or ideas
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: it was said (to لَهُمُ - to them)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that something was done to someone instead of someone doing it. This connects to لَهُمُ (to them) in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the command 'fear/be mindful' (ٱتَّقُوا۟) that follows.

steps.m: to them (masculine plural, connects to ٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Adds هُمُ (meaning 'them') to لَ (meaning 'to/for') to show this applies to a group of men or mixed group

steps.m: to protect oneself or guard against harm

steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The و changed to ت and got doubled (shadda)
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added ا at start to help pronunciation
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the phrase after it. In this ayah, it's used to refer to 'what' is in front of them (literally: between their hands).

steps.m: between (what is in front of أَيْدِيكُمْ)
steps.n: Common preposition showing location or relationship between things. In this ayah, it refers to what is between/in front of 'your hands' (أَيْدِيكُمْ).
Forms the basic noun for a single hand
Changes from 'hand' to 'hands' by adding أ at start and changing vowels - this is a broken plural form
Adds كُمْ to show these hands belong to all of you (in this ayah, referring to what is in front of all of you)

steps.m: what (referring to what is before and behind them in this ayah)
steps.n: Basic word meaning 'what' - in this ayah it refers to the consequences/punishment that could come from any direction

steps.m: and what (connects to previous warning to fear what is before them)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this warning about what is behind them to the previous warning about what is before them
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to create a word meaning 'behind'
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb اتقوا (fear/guard against)
Added كُمْ at the end to show this means 'behind you all' - the thing that the people are being told to guard against

steps.m: perhaps/maybe (expressing hope that تُرْحَمُونَ will happen)
steps.n: Basic particle expressing hope or expectation. In this ayah, it expresses hope for Allah's mercy mentioned in تُرْحَمُونَ (you will receive mercy)

steps.m: so that you all (might receive mercy)
steps.n: Adds 'kum' at the end to mean 'you all' (speaking to multiple people)
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to make it passive voice
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ at start and changing vowels. Also changes from 'he' to 'you' as the one receiving mercy
Added ونَ at the end to show this applies to a group
Added fatha to show this is the basic negative/relative particle ما. In this ayah, it works with the verb تَأْتِيهِم to say 'does not come to them'.

steps.m: and not/whatever (connects to previous ayah and negates تَأْتِيهِم)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it. This makes it mean 'and not' instead of just 'not'.
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning to show present tense feminine form. This matches with آيَةٍ (sign) which is feminine in Arabic.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ت, sukun on أ, and kasra on ت

steps.m: she comes to them (آيَةٍ comes to them)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show who receives the action (them)
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or belongs to something else
The shadda shows this word blends with the sound before it when reading. It connects to the word ءَايَةٍ (sign/verse) to show where something comes from.

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'a sign'
Adding tanween kasra (two dots below) shows this is indefinite and in genitive case
The words are the same - just written in two different ways. The second way shows the hamza (ء) separately at the start
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or moves away from. In this ayah, it connects 'sign' (ءَايَةٍ) to 'comes to them' (تَأْتِيهِم).
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (تَأْتِيهِم). This is just for smooth pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Adding the short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) turns the root letters into 'he showed/made clear' in the past tense
Changed from 'to show' into 'a sign' by adding ة at the end and putting an alif in the middle
Making the word plural by replacing ة with ات
The word 'signs' gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end to show it belongs to someone, and two alifs combine into one long alif

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely

steps.m: master who cares for others (belonging to ءَايٰتِ)
steps.n: Added a kasra (ِ) to show this word belongs to or comes after ءَايٰتِ (signs)

steps.m: their master who cares for them (belonging to ءَايٰتِ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this refers to their master
This is the base form of the exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before
Added shadda and alif to strengthen and complete the exception meaning. In this ayah, it's excluding something from the signs (ءَايٰتِ) mentioned before.
Added fathas to make it past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of word where middle letters can change.
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action. This word connects to مُعْرِضِينَ (turning away) to tell us who was turning away.
This is a preposition showing distance or topic

steps.m: away from it (referring to the signs/آيات)
steps.n: Added ها to show what we're moving away from. In this ayah, it refers back to the signs (آيات) that were mentioned.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added أ at beginning and changed middle vowel to make the action more intentional
Changed from past tense verb to active participle (doer) by adding مُ at start and kasra after ر

steps.m: those who turn away (subject of كَانُوا)
steps.n: Added ين to show multiple people and َ at end because it follows كَانُوا (they were)
This is a conditional particle that introduces a situation or condition. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when people are told to spend.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed the vowels to show that something was done to the subject rather than the subject doing it. This word is followed by لَهُمْ (to them) in the ayah.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's connected to what comes after it

steps.m: for them (being addressed in قِيلَ - it was said)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. This word connects to قِيلَ (it was said) to show who was being spoken to.
Connected root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Added أ (hamza) at start to show 'he made/caused to spend' - makes the action more direct and purposeful
Changed from 'he spent' to 'spend!' by changing the middle vowel to make it a command to one person
Added وا suffix to make command plural
Starting with the two separate words: مِن (from) and مَا (what)

steps.m: from what (referring to رَزَقَكُمُ - what He has provided you)
steps.n: When مِن comes before مَا, the ن sound blends into the م, making it stronger (shown by the ّ mark). This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is part of what was said (قَالَ) by those who disbelieved.

steps.m: he provided you all (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who received the provision - it was given to multiple people being spoken to
Added damma to show this is doing the action (nominative case)
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes after fatha - this is a common Arabic spelling rule
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning with a shadda on the following letter to make it specific. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in this ayah, showing who did the disbelieving.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added damma to show this is the doer of the action
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This word is what the disbelievers قَالَ (said) in this ayah.
Starting with the basic relative pronoun meaning 'the one who'
Adding ين to make it refer to multiple people

steps.m: for those who (who are being addressed in كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Adding لِ at the beginning to show purpose or direction. This word refers to the same people mentioned in كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved) earlier in the verse.
Added alif after first letter to strengthen meaning from basic trust to religious belief/faith
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: should we feed? (questioning the disbelievers' statement in وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَنفِقُوا)
steps.n: The letter أ (hamza) was added at the start to turn 'we feed' into a mocking question 'should we feed?'
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'who/whom'. In this ayah, it's the object of the verb أَنُطْعِمُ (should we feed), showing who would be fed.
Basic conditional particle that introduces unlikely or impossible scenarios
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda. This makes the 'if' stronger, like saying 'even if' or 'if indeed'
The middle letter ي changes to ا and we add vowel marks to show past tense. This is a common pattern in Arabic for words with ي in the middle.
Changed from past tense (he wanted) to present tense (he wants) by adding يَ at the start
Added long alif to show ongoing/extended wanting
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Added alif at beginning to show causing someone else to eat (making the action happen to others). This changes it from just eating to making others eat.
Added هُ at the end to show who receives the feeding. This word appears in a question asking 'Should we feed whom Allah could feed if He willed?'
Changed the suffix هُ to هُۥٓ which is just a spelling variation that means the same thing
This is a negative particle used here to tell the disbelievers they are not something. It works with أَنتُمْ (you) to form the complete meaning 'you are not'

steps.m: you all (subject of the implied verb 'are')
steps.n: This is a complete second person masculine plural pronoun. In this ayah, it's being used as the subject of an implied verb meaning 'are'. The full meaning would be 'you all are'.

steps.m: except (connects to فِى ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ - in clear error)
steps.n: This is an exceptive particle that shows something is excluded from what was mentioned before. Here it connects to the phrase after it 'in clear error', completing the meaning that 'you are nothing except in clear error'.

steps.m: in (location word related to ضَلٰلٍ - misguidance)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it shows they are 'in' a state of misguidance.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Added alif to show this is a complete or intense form of wandering off

steps.m: in complete error (describing the state of those who rejected in this ayah)
steps.n: The word got 'in' added to it (shown by the two dots under) and uses a special long 'aa' sound (the straight line on top) to say someone is 'in complete error'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show something that does the action of making things clear
The shadda on م makes the meaning stronger
The tanween kasra shows this word describes ضَلٰلٍ (error) in the phrase 'in clear error'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. The middle و changes to ا in this pattern.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter returns to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Basic question word asking about time
Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) to show the extended sound at the end. In this ayah, the disbelievers are asking mockingly about when Allah's promise will come.
Basic pointing word used to indicate something
Added ه at beginning to get attention and show something is near
Added alif mark (ٰ) over ه and fatha on ذ to help pronunciation. This word is pointing to 'the promise' (الْوَعْدُ) that comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment

steps.n: Root letters combined with vowels to make the noun meaning 'a promise' instead of the verb 'to promise'
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific promise
Added ُ to show this word is the subject being asked about in the question 'When is this promise?'

This is a basic word that shows something depends on something else, like saying 'if you study, you'll learn' in English. Here it connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) to show what they're saying depends on something.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'in'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to command form by dropping the middle letter و and changing vowels - telling something to exist/be
Added تُ at the end to change from a command ('be!') to past tense meaning 'I was'

steps.m: you all were (addressing multiple people in يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end - now it's talking to a group instead of one person
Basic past tense verb form - added short vowels (fatha) to make 'he told the truth'
Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. The alif changed to a special mark (ٰ) above the ص.
Added fatha at the end because this word comes after إِن كُنتُمْ (if you are) in the ayah

This word is being used to negate the verb يَنظُرُونَ (they are waiting/looking), making the meaning 'they are not waiting/looking'. In this ayah, it's showing that the disbelievers won't have to wait long before the Day of Judgment comes.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he looked) to present tense (he looks) by adding يَ at the start
Adds masculine plural subject marker to make verb plural
This is the basic form of the exception particle that limits what comes before it. In this ayah, it's limiting what they are waiting for (ما ينظرون) to just one thing.

steps.m: except (limiting what they are waiting for in ما ينظرون)
steps.n: The word إِلَّا is actually one complete word meaning 'except'. It's not really a transformation - إِلَّ without the ا is not a valid separate word in Arabic. The word إِلَّا is used here to limit what comes after the verb ينظرون (they are waiting for).
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of shouting
Added tanween to show this is the object being waited for in the phrase 'they await only...'
Changes from basic root to active participle form, meaning 'one who is unique/united'

steps.m: one (describing a feminine thing that is alone)
steps.n: Added ة at the end to make it describe feminine things
Added tanween to show this describes the word صَيْحَةً (blast) in the ayah. Also changed the alif to an alif with overbar for writing style.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ and changing vowels
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being taken (them)

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence from what happened before

steps.m: to be able, to have power to do something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or gaining ability
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels from طَا to طِي

steps.m: they are able (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are the subject
Changed middle vowel to kasra to prepare for noun formation
Added تَ at start and ة at end to make it a noun for one instance of the action
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'they cannot [make] a recommendation'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Extended the alif to emphasize the negation

steps.m: and not (connects to يَسْتَطِيعُونَ - they are able)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement. This shows that they can't do two things: they can't make a will (testament) AND they can't return to their people.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or destination
Added an alif maksura (ى with a small mark above it) at the end, which is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added a small vertical mark (ٓ) above the alif maksura to show it's being stretched in pronunciation. This connects it smoothly to the next word أَهْلِهِمْ (their families)

steps.m: to settle, to be familiar with

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make the common word for 'family'
Added kasra to connect it to what comes after
Added هِمْ to show it's their family. This word is connected to the verb يَرْجِعُونَ (they return) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the action was done to something rather than by someone. This connects to 'into the horn' (فِى ٱلصُّورِ) that follows it.

steps.m: and it was blown into (فِى ٱلصُّورِ)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this event with previous events in the sequence
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the blowing happens inside the horn (الصُّورِ).
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is the standard spelling of this preposition.
Added damma to create the noun form meaning 'horn/trumpet'
Added the definite article ٱل and shadda on ص because it comes after ٱل
Added kasra at the end because this word follows the preposition في (in/with) in the ayah
This is a basic particle that shows something happens at a specific time or suddenly. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when the Horn is blown.

steps.m: and then suddenly (after وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after the Horn is blown. It connects this event to what was mentioned before.
This is the basic form of the pronoun that refers to a single male or thing

steps.m: they (referring to those rising from graves)
steps.n: Adding م changes the pronoun from singular to plural. In this ayah, it refers to people who will rise from their graves at the sound of the trumpet on the Day of Judgment.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from the house' in English
The shadda (ّ) is added on the م because it comes after certain words that cause this sound to be emphasized. This doesn't change the meaning.
The fatha (َ) is added at the end to connect this word smoothly to the next word ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ (the graves), showing they're linked in meaning - 'from the graves'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic singular noun
Changed to broken plural pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple graves
Added 'the' and genitive case ending ِ because it follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it connects 'their Lord' (رَبِّهِمْ) to show where they are going.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a complete word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
The doubled middle letter (shadda) transforms this into a title or role for someone who does this action extensively
Adding kasra shows this lord belongs to or is connected to something
Adding هِ shows the lord belongs to him. This word follows إِلَىٰ (towards) in the ayah.
Adding masculine plural possessive suffix to indicate multiple possessors
Added يَ at beginning to show action happening now, with ِ on middle letter to show type of action

steps.m: they are rushing out (from الْأَجْدَاثِ/their graves)
steps.n: Added ون to show many people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to people rushing out from their graves when the trumpet is blown.
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The و from the root changes to ا in this word.
Added وا at the end to show many people (all male or mixed group) did the action. Changed the vowel before وا to ُ (damma).
This is the basic word for extreme distress or misfortune
Added يٰ at the beginning to call attention to the distress, like saying 'Oh!' or 'Alas!'
Added نَا at the end to show this woe belongs to us, like saying 'our woe'
This is an interrogative pronoun (اسم استفهام) that has no root since it is a particle/pronoun. It is used to ask 'who?' in questions.
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: who (asking about who woke them up from their sleep)
steps.n: Added a small noon mark (called tanween) that shows this word connects to what comes after it. In this ayah, people are asking 'Who woke us from our sleep?'

steps.m: to send forth, raise up, resurrect

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past

steps.m: he raised/resurrected us (from مَّرْقَدِنَا - our sleeping place)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show that 'we' are the ones being raised. This connects to مَّرْقَدِنَا (our sleeping place) later in the ayah, showing people are being raised from their graves.
This is the basic form of the word that shows where something starts from
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly. In this ayah, it's connecting 'from' to 'our sleeping place' (مَّرْقَدِنَا)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show this is a place where the action happens
Added kasra at end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added نَا at end to show this belongs to 'us'. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah
Doubles the م to add emphasis to the word
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something. It's one of the special words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Adding ه at the beginning makes it specifically point to something nearby, like saying 'this one right here'

steps.m: this (pointing to what the disbelievers are saying about their resurrection)
steps.n: Adding the alif (ا) at the end and the small alif (ٰ) above the ه helps with pronunciation and shows this is the complete form. In this ayah, the disbelievers are using this word to point to their resurrection, expressing surprise.
Basic form of the interrogative particle that asks about something
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation

steps.m: to make a promise about something in the future

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to صَدَقَ (proved true) in this ayah, showing that what was promised came true.
Root letters ر-ح-م turned into the noun رَحْمَ (mercy) by adding vowel marks
Adding ان makes the word mean 'full of mercy' or 'extremely merciful'
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning. The ل combines with the ر to make a stronger ر sound (shown by ّ)
Added ُ at the end to show this is the doer of the action in وَعَدَ (promised). The alif is written specially (ٰ) because this is a divine name.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, this refers to how Allah spoke truth about the resurrection.

steps.m: and he spoke truth (about the resurrection)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about what الرحمن (The Most Merciful) promised
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb 'sent' into 'someone who was sent' by adding مُ at start
Adds masculine sound plural suffix to make the noun plural
Adding the definite article ال makes the word definite ('the messengers' instead of just 'messengers')
This is an emphasis particle that makes the statement stronger. In this ayah, it emphasizes that something definitely was (كَانَتْ) a certain way.
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb

steps.m: she was (part of صَيْحَةً وٰحِدَةً - a single cry)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine. This word describes صَيْحَةً (cry) which is feminine.
This is the base form of the exception particle. It consists of إِ (hamza with kasra) and لّ (lām with shadda).
Added ا (alif) at the end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it negates the word صَيْحَةً (cry), showing there was only one cry, not multiple.

steps.m: to shout or cry out loudly

steps.n: Adding ة makes it mean 'a single instance of shouting'
Added tanween to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. This word is what 'it was' (كَانَتْ) is referring to.
Changes from basic root to active participle form, meaning 'one'
Added ة to show this describes a feminine thing
Added ً to show this describes the word صَيْحَةً (blast) in the ayah
Changed وَا to وٰ which is just a different way of writing the same sound
This is a basic particle that shows something happening suddenly or at a specific time
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with what comes before it. This word connects to مُحْضَرُونَ (they will be brought) to show when this action happens.

This is a separate pronoun meaning 'they' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers to the people who will be brought before Allah, as shown by the word مُحْضَرُونَ (being brought) that comes after it.

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Adding ي between the letters with the vowels makes it describe a complete group rather than the act of gathering

steps.m: all together (describing the group in هُمْ/they)
steps.n: The ٌ shows this word is describing the group mentioned before it (هُمْ/they)
This is a basic word meaning 'at' or 'with' that shows location or presence. It's like saying something is right there with you.

steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this sudden event with what came before it. Shows that one thing leads to another, like saying 'and then suddenly' in English.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it connects to خَصِيمٌ (opponent/disputer) to show who is doing the disputing.
Forms an adjective/noun describing one who disputes/argues frequently
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'something/someone that makes clear'
The م has a shadda because it merged with another م before it, making the sound stronger
Added tanween to show this is describing something in a general way

steps.m: to set forth (an example or comparison)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense. This word is connected to قَالَ (he said) later in the ayah.

steps.m: and he set forth (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this action with what came before
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for us (connected to ضَرَبَ - he set forth)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show this is happening to or for 'us'. This word connects to ضَرَبَ (he set forth) to show he set forth something for us.

steps.m: to be like or set an example

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a noun meaning 'example'

steps.m: an example (object of ضَرَبَ - he set forth)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the thing being acted upon in the sentence 'he set forth an example'
Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'

steps.m: and he forgot (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this with the previous action. This word describes what the person did before he said something (قَالَ).

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something from nothing
creation (the act or result of creating)
steps.n: Changed from verb to noun meaning the act or result of creation
Added هُ to show possession ('his'). The ۥ is just a pronunciation mark showing the vowel is lengthened. This word is what is being forgotten in the phrase 'he forgot his creation'.
Added vowels to show this is about something that happened in the past. This word connects to the next part where someone asks 'who gives life?'
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This makes the word easier to say.

steps.m: who (asks about the person who will give life to the bones)
steps.n: Question word asking about who does something. In this ayah, it's asking who will give life to the bones mentioned in the next part (يُحْىِ ٱلْعِظٰمَ).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added يُ at the beginning to make it present tense and changed the pattern to make it mean 'causes/makes something to live' instead of just 'lived'
Changed the spelling of final ي to ى, which is just a writing variation with no meaning change
Root meaning 'big' becomes plural noun 'bones' - like breaking one bone into many pieces

steps.m: the bones (object of يُحْىِ - gives life to)
steps.n: Added the definite article 'the' and the َ ending to show this word receives the action of 'giving life to'
This is the basic feminine pronoun used to refer to something feminine, in this case referring to العِظٰمَ (the bones)
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, showing this is part of a sequence of thoughts
Added ي between the last two root letters and vowel marks to show something that has completely decayed. This describes the state of the bones (العِظٰمَ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added tanween dammah (two damma marks) at the end to make the word indefinite, meaning 'a decayed thing' rather than 'the decayed thing'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to يُحْيِيهَا - He gives life to it)
steps.n: Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command: Put a u-mark on ق, put a stop-mark on ل, and remove the middle و. This tells the Prophet what to say about Allah giving life to dead bones.
Connected the root letters with sukun and kasra to prepare for the Form IV pattern
Added يُ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present/future and is being done by 'he' (referring to الَّذِي - the One who)

steps.m: he gives life to it (referring to bones/earth)
steps.n: Added ها to show what is receiving the life - referring back to the bones/earth being discussed
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'The person who came' or 'The book that I read'
Adding ٱلّ at the beginning makes it point to something specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'

steps.m: he created it/her (referring to the first creation mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Adding ها refers back to the first creation mentioned earlier in the verse
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Added a shadda (double letter) to show this means 'first time' rather than just 'first'. Like saying 'the very first' instead of just 'first'

steps.m: to pass by or move through

steps.n: Adding the fatha (َ) mark shows this is a past tense verb
Added ة to show one specific instance of the action
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon as part of the phrase 'first time'

steps.m: the one who/which (connects to جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning which makes it more specific. The ل combines with ذ to make a stronger sound (shown by ّ). The ي at the end shows it's talking about one male thing or person.

steps.m: the one who/which (connects to جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning, just the spelling.

steps.m: he made (subject of the verb that connects to لَكُم - for you)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word connects to لَكُم (for you) to show who the action was done for.

steps.m: for you all (receivers of جَعَلَ - made)

Added 'the' to refer back to الشَّجَرِ (the tree) mentioned earlier in the ayah

The ًا at the end shows this is indefinite (any fire) and is being acted upon. This word is what Allah made (جَعَلَ) from green trees.

Added فَ at the beginning to connect this to what came before, showing that one thing follows another, like saying 'and then' or 'so then' in English
Basic pronoun form meaning 'you' when speaking to one person

The ل gets a shadda (double letter mark) and the first alif becomes a waṣla (connecting alif). This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: yes indeed (contradicting the negative question أَوَلَيْسَ - is not?)

Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a result of Allah's command كُن (Be!)
